lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The admin corridor runs straight — service end to executive wing — and Reza knows the schedule. Board meeting breaks at twenty-two hundred. The exit route is predictable. He chose this spot eleven minutes ago.

lines.speaker: DR. REZA SHIRAZI
lines.text: Don't rush it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The folder under his arm is real. A consult request, two pages, nothing urgent. Something a second-year resident might carry anywhere without explanation. He waits near the supply station, watching the far end of the wing.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Footsteps. Measured ones. A large man moving with the ease of someone who owns the building's upper levels — not Harte's ease, which is territorial, which is performed — but the settled ease of a man who has been here long enough that the institution shaped itself around him. Dr. Ellison Marsh. Attending, twenty-eight years. Board member, the last six.

lines.speaker: DR. REZA SHIRAZI
lines.text: Dr. Marsh.

lines.speaker: DR. ELLISON MARSH
lines.text: Dr. Shirazi.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza falls into step. Natural. Incidental. Two people heading the same direction.

lines.speaker: DR. REZA SHIRAZI
lines.text: I've been going through some old intern-year documentation. Routine — for the review process. I came across a notation I didn't recognize. Thought I'd ask around.

lines.speaker: DR. ELLISON MARSH
lines.text: What kind of notation?

lines.speaker: DR. REZA SHIRAZI
lines.text: Chart entry. Fourth floor. Four years back. The handwriting wasn't mine and wasn't the attending of record.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Marsh keeps walking. The pace doesn't change. That's the first thing.

lines.speaker: DR. ELLISON MARSH
lines.text: Charts from four years ago are archived. If there's a discrepancy, the records office handles it.

lines.speaker: DR. REZA SHIRAZI
lines.text: Of course. I only wondered if someone might remember the night in question. February. Busy stretch.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Marsh stops. Not at a door, not for any visible reason. A man stops when a calculation completes.

lines.speaker: DR. ELLISON MARSH
lines.text: Every February is a busy stretch, Dr. Shirazi.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He says the name with a particular precision — not surprise, not quite wariness. The care of a man who has been moving it from one private file to another for a while. A name gets said with that weight when it has already come up somewhere else, in a different room, and this encounter confirms what was already suspected.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza hears it.

lines.speaker: DR. REZA SHIRAZI
lines.text: Right. Of course. I appreciate the time.

lines.speaker: DR. ELLISON MARSH
lines.text: Good luck with the review.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Marsh moves on. Even pace. Without hesitation — which is its own answer.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Further down the wing, the elevator bank light shifts. Harte steps out — unhurried, phone at his ear, not registering Reza at all. He turns toward the east office suites. His business is his own.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza stands alone under the fluorescent lights. The folder is still under his arm. The question was oblique enough that nothing was stated. Nothing was admitted. A reasonable man could call the exchange unremarkable.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: But Marsh spoke the name with weight before Reza had explained why he was asking. Said it the way a name gets said when it has already been moved — considered, placed, filed — in whatever room Marsh inhabits when the building goes quiet.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza turns toward the ER wing. The chart entry is real. The handwriting is Marsh's. And Marsh knows Reza has found it. Three facts. Enough to decide what comes next. Not enough to survive it alone.
